headline: Revenue opened the year at AED 45,440 across 320 orders, with a blended ROAS of 3.06x.
what_improved: Repeat customer rate started at a solid 35.9%, a healthy base for a young D2C brand.
TikTok delivered the strongest channel ROAS at 2.9x on a small test budget.
what_declined: Conversion rate sat at 1.76%, leaving clear room for funnel improvement.
why: January reflects a baseline month: acquisition-led, with Meta and Google carrying most spend while organic and retail quietly contributed a third of revenue.
recommended_actions: Hold Meta and Google budgets steady while gathering a full month of clean attribution.
Increase TikTok test budget modestly given its leading ROAS.
Begin a basic post-purchase email flow to lift the repeat rate further.
budget_reallocation: Shift ~10% of Google spend into TikTok where ROAS is materially higher.
product_opportunity: Healthy Bread Bundle leads at ~22% of revenue and is the natural acquisition hero.
retention_recommendation: Launch a welcome email series to convert first-time buyers into a second order.

headline: Revenue grew to AED 52,925 (+16.5% MoM) on 365 orders as retention began compounding.
what_improved: Repeat rate rose to 39.7%, up nearly 4 points month over month.
Blended ROAS improved to 3.16x while spend grew only modestly.
what_declined: TikTok ROAS slipped from 2.9x to 2.7x — the first sign of early creative fatigue.
why: Growth came from a healthier mix: returning customers grew faster than new, which is the most profitable way to scale.
recommended_actions: Refresh TikTok creative with 2-3 new angles before increasing budget.
Scale Meta prospecting, which held a stable ~2.0x ROAS.
Introduce a simple bundle upsell at checkout to support AOV.
budget_reallocation: Keep TikTok flat pending creative refresh; direct new budget to Meta.
product_opportunity: High Protein Bread is gaining share and signals a premium-positioning opportunity.
retention_recommendation: Add a 14-day second-order incentive for January's new cohort.

headline: Revenue reached AED 63,640 (+20.2% MoM); conversion climbed to 1.98%.
what_improved: Conversion rate improved to 1.98% as funnel friction eased.
Repeat rate reached 41.9%, and a VIP segment (3+ orders) began to emerge.
what_declined: TikTok ROAS continued to ease to 2.5x; the refresh has not fully landed.
why: The funnel is converting better and the customer base is maturing — VIP customers appearing this early is a strong retention signal.
recommended_actions: Formalise a VIP tier with early access and a loyalty perk.
Test a higher-AOV bundle aimed at returning customers.
Continue iterating TikTok creative; treat it as a brand-reach channel if ROAS stabilises near 2.4x.
budget_reallocation: Maintain channel mix; protect Meta scaling which is the efficiency anchor.
product_opportunity: High Protein Bread now rivals the Bundle — consider a protein-led bundle.
retention_recommendation: Activate a win-back flow for the January cohort approaching lapse.

headline: Revenue grew to AED 76,255 (+19.8% MoM) on 505 orders; repeat rate crossed 45%.
what_improved: Returning customers (230) nearly matched new (275) — a maturing retention engine.
Meta ROAS strengthened to 2.2x as prospecting scaled efficiently.
what_declined: CAC ticked up to AED 83.5 as acquisition pushed into broader audiences.
why: The brand is scaling acquisition while holding efficiency — CAC remains far below AOV (AED 151), so growth stays profitable.
recommended_actions: Hold the current Meta scaling pace; watch CAC against a AED 100 ceiling.
Double down on email, which is now contributing meaningful revenue.
Plan a May promotion around the protein range to lift AOV.
budget_reallocation: Allow a measured spend increase on Meta; keep TikTok as supporting reach.
product_opportunity: Protein and Oats breads are rising together — a 'high-performance' bundle could land well.
retention_recommendation: Segment email by purchase history to push relevant repeat offers.

headline: Revenue accelerated to AED 95,480 (+25.2% MoM); repeat rate reached 48.4%.
what_improved: Strongest MoM growth of the period so far at +25.2%.
Blended ROAS held at 3.39x despite a meaningful spend increase.
what_declined: TikTok ROAS fell to 2.2x; it is now a marginal paid channel.
CAC rose to AED 87.9 as volume scaled.
why: Momentum is real and broad-based, but TikTok efficiency has eroded enough to warrant a budget decision rather than another creative test.
recommended_actions: Reallocate the majority of TikTok paid budget to Meta prospecting.
Prepare for June peak with inventory and creative ready in advance.
Introduce a loyalty milestone to push more customers into the VIP tier.
budget_reallocation: Move ~60% of TikTok paid budget to Meta; keep a small TikTok presence for reach.
product_opportunity: Protein Bread is close to overtaking the Bundle as the top revenue product.
retention_recommendation: Reward third purchases to accelerate VIP conversion.

headline: Revenue reached AED 120,080 — up 25.8% MoM and 2.6x since January.
what_improved: Repeat rate hit a period-high of 52.6%; returning customers (400) now exceed new (360).
Conversion reached 2.40% and blended ROAS improved to 3.48x.
what_declined: TikTok ROAS bottomed at 2.1x; CAC rose to AED 95.8 at peak scale.
why: Growth is now retention-led rather than purely acquisition-driven — the healthiest possible position. The rising CAC is an expected cost of scaling and remains well below AOV (AED 158).
recommended_actions: Shift the remaining TikTok paid budget to Meta, where CAC is materially lower.
Refresh TikTok with three new UGC angles before any re-scaling.
Launch a win-back email flow targeting one-time buyers from Q1.
budget_reallocation: Move ~15% of total paid budget from TikTok to Meta prospecting; hold Google steady.
product_opportunity: High Protein Bread is now 22% of revenue and the #1 product — bundle it with Oats Bread to lift AOV.
retention_recommendation: Introduce a second-order discount triggered 14 days after first purchase to convert the growing new-customer base.
